content: I actually want to object, "Aigis" is the original Greek transcription of the same word, so it has rights to exist. The way Achilles is originally Akhilleus and Chiron is Kheiron. And it probably fits the case better because "Aigis" is a more obscure version of the very same term that's actually closer to its culture of origin. The obscurity here works specifically because the word "aegis" is in the active English vocabulary and that's, like,  supposed to be her given name. It sounds less like a proper noun if one uses a commonplace word
oh cool, I didn't know that
that makes more sense then
If you started with a file from my GitHub, upload it back where you found it; otherwise keep using the other system.
gee-whiz
A Dewy-Eyed Thank You Gift
yeah honestly I think innocent is the best option here

or we could go more direct
"A Child's Thanks"
honestly i'd be fine with it
though maybe too direct
yeah, feels too stilted again.
An Innocent Thanks would be effectively the exact same thing but not so literal in-your-face
fair
I'm fine with that
i have typed out the word thanks so many times that it no longer seems like a word and now it feels wrong to me
but yeah honestly I think innocent thanks is about the best we'll get

alright, I picked `An Innocent Thanks`
next up, is "Node" really the word we use for map tiles? seems like we should use "Tile" lol
either seems fine to me
node is pretty common gaming/roguelike terminology but tile is pretty common boardgame/mapping terminology
i have exactly zero strong feelings either way
I guess the Keys in the source file for those *are* called MapNodeType
call it a word of god then
just like not-Aegis
(i am never going to get over this now that i've noticed the text)
it just feels really weird for a "Resonance Node" to describe what happens at a "Node"
oh yeah true, resonance nodes would be for the skill trees
hmmm
what's the chinese terms on both?

it's okay, she's not the only one...
\*points to ~~Leia~~ Leigh\*
honestly Node is pretty consistently only used to refer to map tiles. in-game the literal *nodes* in the skill tree are just called "Resonances".
oh
we could just do that I guess
considering resonance nodes will only ever come up in the context of the resonance tree i don’t think we're really risking all that much confusion if we do that
and it'd keep the map stuff consistent in naming
yup
funnily enough one of my friends I got into the game is a hong konger and plays in chinese because why deal with the MTL if you don't have to
and he calls them all by the obvious romanized names
and when i was like yeah the english calls her leigh he was just like what the fuck why
not to mention Daffodil's name having *literally nothing* to do with Daffodils or flowers at all
he also calls her aegis
for obvious reasons
because aegis is literally the mythological shield used to protect against the gaze of the medusa
you know, the greek mythological entity whose gaze petrifies her foes
that aegis
the one very obviously not aigis
I don’t have a problem with this i swear
Cassius had an A-Tier rant about this a while back lol, because apparently now multiple other languages call her whatever their language's word for Daffodils is
it's okay though, it could be much worse
<:jenkin_processing:1408324289115062333>
lady dovetail it is
oh my god.
I wish I knew more about this character cus I REALLY feel like Dovetail fits WAY better
nah it's pretty explicitly daffodil
chinese transliteration of names don't always match the english pronunciations one to one
that phrase is meant to be daffodil
but yeah I also have not done her story so I also have no clue why she's called what she's called
it might literally just be because she's purple tbh
DAFFODILS ARENT FUCKING PURPLE
no such thing as a naturally purple daffodil, sorry
but yeah it doesn't make instinctive sense to me either but daffodils tend to be correlated with purple things fairly often
i don’t know if it's some pop culture thing or just some mandela effect silliness going on but i've seen daffodil being used in regards to purple quite a few times
this picture is of a crocus <:PAIMON_DEAD:708170044848472105>
sadly, pop culture cares not for your facts and logic
did you know the average human eats 5 spiders a day
Spiders Georg is an outlier and should *not* have been counted

anyways so I'm pondering how I want to template the Resonances that add cards to the deck.
(outside of combat I'll be using "add" as the verb for this, it's short, intuitive and unobtrusive)
I feel like there's plenty of space in this menu to make it look nice, but I am on PC so that might make a difference.
I kinda want to have a clean separation between the description of the Resonance and the description of the Card.

> At the [A Gift of Kindness] node, add the Buff card "An Innocent Thanks" to your deck.
> 
> An Innocent Thanks
> Arithmetica Cost: 1
> Grants 30 Aliemus to the Awakener with the lowest current Aliemus, then is Exhausted.
(the actual card would read properly)
looks fine
just checked this menu on mobile, there's just as much space there if not more
into the style guide it goes~

silly question, but would it be wrong to use unusual brackets/quotes for select uses in English? I'm especially fond of them because I don't see them often at all, but I understand ~~it's because I'm stupid~~ they're much more normal in other languages so "I think they're cool" isn't really valid.
It *feels* like good shape language to use 《Angle Brackets》when referring to map nodes, but technically they're hexagonal the other way around
it would be even better shape language to refer to cards using 「Corner Brackets」
Honestly I like that one so much I'm gonna use it
i enjoy corner brackets aesthetically but neither angle nor corner brackets are commonly used in english
but i also think normal english brackets are super boring and other languages get all the good stuff
so why let them keep all the good stuff for themselves
that's why I feel like for *very specific* uses, seeing them in gameplay elements helps build visual cohesion
i see no issues with using them for the sake of coolness

the real question is, would it be wrong to use the monospace versions of them in a non-monospace language? I don't know why but the monospace brackets feel... better. they take up a lot more space, yes, but that directs attention well.
But, using a space ` ` in addition to the extra space they take up is *too much* space so I'd want to use just the extra space provided. problem is that if they get converted at any point in the process of implementation, then we'd end up with no space at all...

for example
adding ⌈An Innocent Thanks⌋ to the deck feels deflated
compared to adding「An Innocent Thanks」to the deck
literal virgin vs. chad shit
tbh that question probably goes to ace because it depends on how they're parsing and formatting it
like how we're not supposed to use ellipses characters and instead do three manual dots
well, they seem to be using them just fine in every other non-monospace language lol
yeah like i accidentally used ellipses characters in my text (thanks excel) and it seemed to work fine ingame so i assume nothing would *break* but

> Usar 「Regalo inocente」 recupera +10 de locura
> Utiliser 「Retour naïf」 pour +10 de récupération de folie
> Menggunakan 「Hadiah Polos」pemulihan Aliemus +10
direct copy-pastes from the sheet I'm working on
i assume the guidelines were provided for a reason so only Ace could answer as to why
should be fine

alright so how do we like this
> At the〔A Gift of Kindness〕node, add the Buff card「An Innocent Thanks」to your deck.
> 
> An Innocent Thanks
> Arithmetica Cost: 1
> Grants 30 Aliemus to the Awakener with the lowest current Aliemus, then is Exhausted.

on second thought, if I'm citing shape language as a reason to use these, then angle brackets should be reserved for《Orisons》
let's use 〔Tortoise Shells〕for map nodes instead.
never seen those brackets before but they look nice
very fitting for map nodes tbh

So I've got two questions ...
Should we just upload our contributions to Mercy's github for now? I saw that some pull requests from three weeks ago still haven't been merged on the official BIAV github

If I want to split a file into two or three parts, how should I name it? DE_Gameplay(AwakenersPart1)_Proofreading ?
I was wondering re: the first question that's why I've been a little inactive with what I've been working on (+ being sick)

<@&1419930759182942309> I'm curious how best to translate this term:
> 天真的回礼
it's the name of the Buff card that Chapter 1 Resonance gives you. 
Currently it's "Innocent Return Gift" but that doesn't read right.
Thoughts?

innocent return gift is the literal translation, yeah
天真 = innocent
的 = possessive particle
回礼 = return gift, as in like the gift you give in return for receiving a gift (on second thought, this isn't really a common cultural thing in america is it? I'm having a hard time thinking of an immediate cultural equivalent)
I guess the key thing here is to consider how it's tied to the context of the chapter 1 story because that's the context it should be presented in
回礼 could be return manners I guess, like the way you give thanks back
but I can't think of how that would be relevant to the chapter 1 story....
chapter 1 was aigis? did she ever give you something back as a return gift...
I remember the skill mats were her candies but I don't recall if that was relevant to the story context
If it helps, here's the Event that gives it to you.
ah okay yeah, so it's literally the candy she gives you as a return gift as thanks
here's the Activation Material
minor gripe but
this could not be more clearly Aegis
so why is it Aigis
lmao
Aegis is the most common romanization anyway and the obviously accurate one so who on earth decided it was Aigis
and why are we sticking to it when there's evidence *right there*

grumble grumble something workhouse is obviously objectively inferior to almshouse as a term but just because the cg has english spelling on it we have to translate it that way grumble AND YET AEGIS SPELLING IS RIGHT THERE
anyway rant aside, hmm
"An innocent thank you gift" would probably be the most accurate? but obviously would not fit as a card name given the length
So diegetically the card that's added to our deck is the card that was in the box, the one with our stick figures on it that says "Thank you"
god why is this game so mean to the children
my poor baby aigis didn't deserve to suffer like this
jeez
so what would we describe that card as...?
conceptually it might be most fitting to name it "Innocent Thanks"?
for some reason my head can't get past "A Thanks in Return" but that's just straight up not a correct translation
well no that sounds a bit weird
"Innocent Gift"? since the concept of a return gift isn't really as culturally relevant in the west I don't think
A Gift of Innoc-
(for legal reasons this is a joke)
lmao
honestly yeah there's not really a good option here
because english just doesn't really have a singular term for return gift
literally it would be a "Letter of Thanks" or a "Thank-You Card"
those are pretty common at least in normal-people America

your options are either going to be
- Innocent Return Gift (awkward, doesn't convey well but is the literal translation)
- Innocent Gift (drops the return because of lacking cultural context but at least still manages to convey the innocence and that it's a gift)
- Thank You Gift (dropping the innocence because it just doesn't really apply well as an adjective in english compared to how well it flows in chinese, but you literally just removed half of the meaning of the original term)
wait
reciprocation
An Innocent Reciprocation?
hmmm
good, but too long
Innocent Reciprocation without the an?
that's probably pushing the limits there but I'm pretty sure there are longer cards
I guess? technically that is how long the card's name already is lmao
yeah true
the innocence here could also be replaced with something meaningfully equivalent
also potentially relevant is that the actual Resonance node is called "(A) Gift of Kindness"
it's not like innocent is mandatory, that might be sticking too much to the exact definition
oh yeah let me check if that event is called the same
maybet here's a textual symmetry there
here I got it
善意的礼物
nope it's literally a gift of kindness
what else works for innocent....
here it's being used specifically to convey childlike innocence
naivety? childlike? pure
Honestly I don't see anything wrong with localizing to "A Thanks in Return" considering the diegetic context
it is literally a "thank you!" in return for helping the workhouse kids
yeah that's just the third option above where it abides by the contextual meaning but you are technically losing literally half of the entire original phrase
yeah... 😅
it's just kind of annoying because 回礼 in chinese implies thank you because of cultural context
which is missing in english and thus not really translateable in a short form
so yeah some amount of meaning must be lost either way, it's just picking and choosing which of the 3 components you want to lose
the innocence, the return, or the gift
imo the return is probably the least valuable one since even without being explicitly stated, it's still quite clear in both the event and the natural context that it's a return thank you gift
so just An Innocent Gift?
naive honestly feels like it could be a good word but unfortunately that just has too much negative contextual implications in english so it's not going to work imo
Could we find a suitable synonym for innocent? I was about to mention naive and reject it but we should keep looking
Naïve Thanks would be like the closest but the word naive in english honestly just has too much of an implication of simpleminded or exploitability to it so I don't think it works
"A Simple Thanks"?
simple has too many meanings too
"A Sincere Thanks"?
runs the risk of interpreting it in a way outside of the childlike meaning
sincere doesn't imply innocence I don't think, it's just an emphatic on the thanks
childlike is the only other alternative word I can think of
i
i don't think kawaii is an english word
and regardless of what evidence is raised against me i will refuse to acknowledge it
I always check Merriam first, but Thesaurus.com is valid when I don't find what I know I'm looking for
Honest?
An Honest Thanks?
same problem as Sincere
hmm it feels like it's missing something
yeah
